info.title: OPG Transaction Initiation
info.description: The OPG 1.0 API supports the creation of 'payment initiation URLs'.

post.description: This service is mainly useful to initiate a payment and actively redirect the browser to the payment page. As such, a payment session is initiated and persisted in OPG.  Not intended for batch processing.

parameters.name: merchant_id
parameters.description: The Merchant identifier as defined in OPG.

requestBody.description: A PayableItem's for which to initiate a payment.

301.description: Successful call.  Redirect to the actual Payment Initiation URL.  At this point, a payment session has been created, and persisted, in OPG.

post.description: This service is provided to generate a batch of URLs. Per payable item, three URLs are returned:  (1) The payment-initiation URL, (2) A payment-status URL, and (3) A payment-status URL, pointing to a image representation.  The purpose is oriented towards (batch) preparation of outbound emails or static web screens.

parameters.name: merchant_id
parameters.description: The Merchant identifier as defined in OPG.

requestBody.description: A list of PayableItem's for which to generate URLSet's.  Each PayableItem is associated with a requestLabel, so that the resulting URLSet's can be linked back to the requests.  (Implementation issue: the service does not take an array of PayableItem's, but simple 'list' of PayableItem's.)

200.description: Successful call.  The response body contains a list of URLSets, corresponting with the PayableItems in the request.  (Implementation issue: the service does not return an array of URLSet's, but simple 'list' of URLSet's.)

merchantId.description: The identifier that OPG uses to identify the merchant that will receive the payment.  Same as "merchant_id" in the path.

merchantDocumentId.description: Unique identifier for the item being paid (typically an invoice), assigned by the calling system.

accountSelector.description: For merchants with multiple accounts this corresponds with the accountSelector configured in OPG which maps to the actual account at the PSP. By convention the accountSelector "DEFAULT" is used when it is likely there will only ever be one account.

paymentAmount.description: The amount to be paid.  Notice: this amount must use the "dot" as the decimal, and no thousands separators are permitted.

referenceMessage.description: Reference, choosen by the biller, to be passed on to the PSP. Typically this appears on customer statements and is used for reconciliation purposes.

description.description: Description of the subject of the payment.

confirmationEmailAddress.description: Email address for email communication with the payer. Typically used for emails for confirmation of Payment. Note that if confirmation is required, a flow needs to be configured in UM Server.

payerLanguageCode.description: Two digit ISO language code. Used to provide UI and email localization.

documentNumber.description: Number of the document (typically an invoice number or some other identifier) as it is known to the merchant. Typically part of the hash that makes up the MerchantDocumentId. Does not need to be unique.

accountNumber.description: Identifier of a customer account (typically how the end-customer is known in the merchant ERP system.)  Does not need to be unique.

successRedirectUrl.description: The redirect URL in case of Successful Payment. If you need parameters then these can be supplied in the URL. WARNING this will be provided to the client browser so no sensitive information should be exposed here. Additonally any "identifiers" must be random and not sequential.

failureRedirectUrl.description: The redirect URL in case of failure (or unknown outcome). In rare cases a payment may have actually taken place but we have not received a confirmation from the PSP, but it should be treated as a failure.

statusChangeNotificationUrlSucces.description: Receives a GET is case of success.

statusChangeNotificationUrlFailure.description: Receives a GET in case of failure.

statusChangeNotificationUrl.description: Receives a POST containing all details about the payment.

properties.description: JSON Map of properties. These are stored with the payment session and passed through to the XML provided to UM Server for the confirmation email.

paymentURL.description: The URL to initiate a payment.

statusURL.description: The URL to retrieve the payment status of a payable item.

statusImageURL.description: The URL to retrieve the payment status of a payable item, in the shape of a dynamic image.

requestLabel.description: The label of the item in the request body.
The label of the item in the request body.
